question_title: Will either Mpox clade 1b or West Nile Virus be detected in U.S. wastewater on August 1, 2026?
summary: The question resolves YES if the WastewaterSCAN dashboard shows any positive samples for Mpox clade 1b OR West Nile Virus through the reporting date on/after Aug 1, 2026. August 1 is near the peak of WNV season, ~140 sites are monitored nationwide, and a retrospective study showed per-site positivity of 3-13% during the summer/fall peak. Even one positive sample suffices, and Mpox clade Ib offers a second path. The 'any samples positive for the period through the reporting date' phrasing also favors a cumulative reading, under which WNV has already had detections (Dec 2025, May 2026). The main residual risk is technical—dashboard display window or accessibility—partly mitigated by the fine-print backup source. Final estimate ~0.97.

factors.description: WNV is strongly seasonal peaking in July-August; Aug 1 is near peak season with ~140 sites monitored nationwide and per-site peak positivity of 3-13%. At-least-one positive is near-certain.
Resolution phrasing 'any samples positive for the period through the reporting date' favors a cumulative reading; WNV already had positives in Dec 2025 and May 2026.
Two independent OR paths (WNV or Mpox clade Ib) both provide a route to YES.
Downside: dashboard may show a narrow rolling window or national aggregate reading 'not-detected', or accessibility issues at resolution (mitigated by backup source).

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.97 (YES)**

West Nile Virus detection in US wastewater around August 1, 2026 is nearly certain. Key reasoning:

- **Peak season timing**: August 1 sits at the peak of WNV season. With ~140 sites monitored nationwide and per-site positivity of 3–13% during the summer/fall peak (per a retrospective study), the probability that at least one site shows a positive is very high.
- **Low bar**: Only a single positive sample is needed to resolve YES.
- **Two independent paths**: Either WNV or Mpox clade Ib detection triggers YES.
- **Cumulative phrasing**: "Any samples positive...for the period through the reporting date" favors an any-positive reading; WNV already had detections in Dec 2025 and May 2026.

The main residual risk is technical (dashboard display window or accessibility), partly mitigated by the fine-print backup source—which keeps this just short of certainty at 0.97.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if the  [WastewaterSCAN Dashboard](https://data.wastewaterscan.org/) shows any samples positive for Mpox clade 1b or West Nile Virus for the period through the reporting date, when checked by Metaculus on or after August 1, 2026.
fine_print: In case of accessibility issues with the main resolution source, Metaculus may check the July [monthly update](https://wwscan.ghost.io/) as a backup source.

factors.description: WNV is strongly seasonal peaking in July-August; August 1 is near peak season with ~140 sites monitored nationwide. Retrospective study shows per-site positivity 3-13% during peak. Probability at least one of ~140 sites positive is near-certain.
Resolution phrasing 'any samples positive for the period through the reporting date' favors cumulative reading; WNV already had positives Dec 2025 and May 2026 in current dashboard history.
Two independent OR paths (WNV or Mpox clade Ib) both provide route to YES.
Downside risk: dashboard may show a narrow rolling window with only 'not-detected' national aggregate, or accessibility issues at resolution.

input.anchor: Base rate for a seasonal endemic pathogen being detected in at least one of ~140 monitored wastewater sites during its peak season: very high, ~90%+.
input.assessment: West Nile Virus detection in US wastewater around August 1 is nearly certain given it is peak WNV season, ~140 sites are monitored, and per-site positivity rates during peak season run 3-13%. Even a single positive sample resolves YES, and the phrasing favors a cumulative any-positive reading. Mpox clade Ib adds a second path. Main residual risk is technical (dashboard display window or accessibility), which the fine print mitigates via a backup source.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor ~90% pushed higher by peak-season timing, large site count, cumulative phrasing, and dual OR paths. Capping near 0.97 to reserve mass for technical/accessibility failure modes.
input.key_uncertainties: Exact display window of the dashboard and whether resolution requires a current-date positive vs any historical positive. Both interpretations still favor strong YES.
input.tool_audit: research() returned comprehensive, well-sourced findings confirming monitoring status, seasonality, and historical positivity rates. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was highly effective for this seasonal-detection question, returning exactly the data points needed (monitoring start dates, positivity rates, recent detections). No friction; a single research call was sufficient for a straightforward near-certain question.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All four factors are grounded in the research trace and worldview research entry (does-wastewaterscan-dashboard-test-for-west-nile-v-9b995523, state=fresh):

- Factor 1 (WNV seasonal, ~140 sites, 3-13% per-site peak positivity): Grounded. Trace confirms ~140 sites monitored (139 not-detected + positives in May 2026) and the retrospective study (PMC12296570) showing per-site positivity of 3.3% Sacramento, 5.9% Woodland, 13% Lincoln NE during summer/fall peak. Jul-Aug WNV peak is standard epidemiology; the study confirms detectability during that peak.
- Factor 2 (cumulative reading; WNV positives Dec 2025 + May 2026): Grounded — trace confirms 1 detection in Iowa Dec 2025 and 1 nationally May 2026.
- Factor 3 (two OR paths): Correct structural fact.
- Factor 4 (downside: narrow rolling window / accessibility): Legitimate risk, correctly signed negative, and mitigated by the fine-print backup source.

No hallucinations, no double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no assessment contradictions.

Resolution criteria WERE provided and the agent engaged with the specific terms: the WastewaterSCAN Dashboard source, the "any samples positive... through the reporting date" phrasing, and the ghost.io backup source. Good alignment.

Pre-publication event check: The Dec 2025/May 2026 positives are used as base-rate/cumulative evidence, not treated as already resolving YES. Forecast is forward-looking from published_at (Jul 4) to resolution (Aug 1), targeting peak-season detection. No Case 2 violation.

Counterargument is genuinely strong, not a token gesture — it builds a coherent alternative grounded in real observed data (135 not-detected Oct 2025, 139 not-detected May 2026 with only 1 positive; brand-new monitoring launched mid-Oct 2025; possible low 2026 season; national-aggregate display could read as "not detected"). Confidence self-assessment (0.85) appropriately acknowledges the unproven-first-prospective-season uncertainty.

Tool audit ("No failures") matches ground truth (11 calls, 0 errors).

Independent probability check: With per-site peak positivity of ~3-13% across ~140 sites and multiple collections through July at peak season, the probability of at least one positive site by Aug 1 is very high; the dominant residual risk is technical/display (Factor 4), mitigated by the backup source. I would land ~93-97%, consistent with the agent's 97%. The one mild caution — WNV wastewater monitoring is brand-new and national aggregates have frequently read NOT-DETECTED even during monitored months — is legitimately captured in Factor 4 and the 0.85 confidence, so I don't see a meaningful mispricing. No change warranted.
